purpose: Synthetic sample data for showing comprehensive Spectrum human-AI conversation reports across common voice-agent domains.

top_conversation_types.why_it_matters: High-volume support calls are the most common place teams test voice agents because the intent set is repetitive but user frustration is visible when resolution fails.
Scheduling calls have clear success criteria, system-of-record dependencies, and measurable failure modes such as no-shows or unresolved booking.

top_conversation_types.type: Outbound sales follow-up and lead qualification
top_conversation_types.why_it_matters: Warm-lead follow-up is a common outbound pattern because timing, consent, qualification, and handoff quality all determine whether automation helps or harms conversion.

top_conversation_types.type: Billing, payment reminders, and collections follow-up
top_conversation_types.why_it_matters: Payment conversations are operationally valuable but sensitive, so the report must surface empathy, compliance, escalation, and clarity risks.
Feedback calls are structured, measurable, and useful for analytics, but they still fail when the agent talks too much or misses sentiment shifts.

sources.label: AGIX Technologies AI voice agents guide
Cresta customer support voice AI use cases
QuickVoice inbound vs outbound AI voice agents guide
VoiceInfra outbound call automation use cases
Deepgram 2025 State of Voice AI report

reports.scenario: Inbound order-status support call where the human wants an exact delivery answer.
reports.expected_user_goal: Get a clear order status and next step in under two minutes.

executive_summary.overall_diagnosis: The agent identified the order-status intent, but delayed the lookup, repeated authentication, and never converted the tracking data into a clear promise.
top_risks: User waited through a long tool gap
executive_summary.recommended_next_action: Inspect the lookup/tool trace and redesign the authentication recovery path before comparing this agent version against production calls.

findings.title: Tool lookup created a visible dead-air gap
findings.claim: The AI took 7200 ms to respond after the user provided the order number.
findings.impact: The user filled the silence with uncertainty and asked whether the agent was still there.
findings.likely_cause: Order lookup or ASR finalization blocked the response without a filler or progress phrase.
findings.suggested_next_check: Replay the gap and check whether the agent can acknowledge lookup progress after 1500 ms.

findings.title: Authentication loop repeated the same request
findings.claim: The AI asked for identity confirmation twice after the user had already provided the required account detail.
findings.impact: The user had to repeat information, which increases abandonment and undermines trust.
findings.likely_cause: The agent did not persist the prior slot fill after a failed lookup response.
findings.suggested_next_check: Check slot memory and validation behavior after the first authentication attempt.

findings.title: Order status was not translated into a customer-ready answer
findings.claim: The agent found a shipping event but ended with an ambiguous 'it should update soon' instead of a concrete next step.
findings.impact: The user still does not know whether to wait, contact support, or expect delivery.
findings.likely_cause: The agent lacked an outcome policy for delayed carrier scans.
findings.suggested_next_check: Define a carrier-delay response policy with a date, escalation threshold, and exact user next step.

conversation_arc.label: Beginning
conversation_arc.summary: The user states a clear order-status goal and the AI recognizes the domain quickly.
details: The greeting and first response are concise.

conversation_arc.label: Middle
conversation_arc.summary: The call degrades during lookup and authentication, where timing and slot memory break the flow.
details: Dead air appears after the order number.

conversation_arc.label: End
conversation_arc.summary: The AI gives a vague shipping explanation and fails to close with a concrete promise.

human_experience.label: Human experience
human_experience.summary: The human starts with a simple goal, then shows uncertainty after silence and repetition.
details: Maximum wait after human turn: 7200 ms.
Two uncertainty markers after the lookup gap.
One explicit 'are you still there?' moment.

agent_behavior.label: Agent behavior
agent_behavior.summary: The agent can identify intent, but lookup progress, slot persistence, and resolution language need work.

trust_limits.label: Human/AI roles
trust_limits.summary: Human and AI speaker roles are clear in the transcript.

trust_limits.label: Tool trace not attached
trust_limits.summary: Likely-cause claims about lookup behavior should be verified against backend trace logs.

transcript.text: Thanks for calling Acme. I can help with order status. What is your order number?
It is AC-1948. I just want to know if it is coming today.
I found the order, but I need to confirm the email on the account again.
I already gave that. It is amal at example dot com. Are you still checking?
The package has a carrier scan from yesterday. It should update soon.

reports.scenario: Outbound appointment reminder where the patient tries to reschedule.
reports.expected_user_goal: Move an appointment to Friday afternoon and receive confirmation.

executive_summary.overall_diagnosis: The agent handled reminder intent but failed the rescheduling task because it offered unavailable slots and did not confirm the final state.
executive_summary.recommended_next_action: Review calendar lookup constraints and require a final confirmation sentence before ending scheduling calls.

findings.title: Agent offered an unavailable slot
findings.claim: The AI offered Friday 3:30 PM, then later said Friday afternoon was unavailable.
findings.impact: Contradictory availability makes the scheduling agent unsafe to automate without review.
findings.likely_cause: The agent proposed from a stale availability cache before receiving calendar confirmation.
findings.suggested_next_check: Force calendar verification before any spoken slot offer.

findings.title: Calendar search pause is too long
findings.claim: The AI paused 6400 ms after the patient asked for Friday afternoon.
findings.impact: The patient interrupted the silence and asked if the agent was checking.
findings.likely_cause: Calendar lookup blocked response generation without a progress update.
findings.suggested_next_check: Add a progress phrase after 1500 ms and measure whether users stop barging in.

findings.title: Appointment state is unresolved at hangup
findings.claim: The call ends without a final confirmed appointment time.
findings.impact: The user may believe the appointment changed even though the system did not confirm it.
findings.likely_cause: The flow lacks an explicit close-state check for rescheduling.
findings.suggested_next_check: Require final state: kept, rescheduled, cancelled, or transferred.

conversation_arc.label: Beginning
conversation_arc.summary: The reminder open is clear and consentful.
details: The patient understands the reason for the call.

conversation_arc.label: Middle
conversation_arc.summary: The call becomes risky when the patient changes the task from reminder to reschedule.
details: The agent speaks before verified availability.

conversation_arc.label: End
conversation_arc.summary: The agent does not close with a confirmed appointment state.

human_experience.label: Human experience
human_experience.summary: The patient is cooperative but loses confidence when availability changes mid-call.

agent_behavior.label: Agent behavior
agent_behavior.summary: The agent is good at reminder scripting but weak at real-time calendar grounding.

trust_limits.label: Calendar trace required
trust_limits.summary: The report can see the contradiction, but exact root cause needs the calendar/tool trace.

transcript.text: Hi, this is Northside Clinic calling to remind you about your appointment tomorrow at 10 AM.
Can I move that to Friday afternoon instead?
I can do Friday at 3:30 PM. Let me confirm that.
Actually Friday afternoon is unavailable. I can keep tomorrow.
Wait, so do I have Friday or not?

reports.scenario: Warm lead follow-up after a buyer requested pricing.
reports.expected_user_goal: Understand whether the product fits and book a human follow-up if qualified.

executive_summary.overall_diagnosis: The agent qualified budget and timing, but over-talked after a buyer objection and failed to secure a clear handoff.
executive_summary.recommended_next_action: Review objection-handling turns and shorten the agent's response before asking for calendar commitment.

findings.title: Agent over-talked after buyer objection
findings.claim: The AI spoke for 31 seconds after the buyer raised a competitor concern.
findings.impact: The buyer's objection was not explored, and the call shifted from discovery into pitch mode.
findings.likely_cause: The agent followed a pitch script instead of switching into objection discovery.
findings.suggested_next_check: Change objection handling to ask one follow-up question before any pitch.

findings.title: Sales handoff was not confirmed
findings.claim: The agent suggested a specialist call but did not confirm a time or owner.
findings.impact: The lead remains qualified but operationally unassigned.
findings.likely_cause: The handoff flow lacks a required booking or routing confirmation.
findings.suggested_next_check: Require the agent to book, route, or explicitly mark follow-up as unconfirmed.

findings.title: Buyer uncertainty increased after pricing answer
findings.claim: The buyer used uncertainty language after the AI described pricing tiers.
findings.impact: Pricing explanation may be too abstract for a voice interaction.
findings.likely_cause: The agent gave tier names instead of mapping price to the buyer's stated team size.
findings.suggested_next_check: Test a pricing answer that gives one range, one assumption, and one next step.

conversation_arc.label: Beginning
conversation_arc.summary: The AI references the form-fill context and gets permission to continue.

conversation_arc.label: Middle
conversation_arc.summary: Discovery starts well, then the AI over-pitches after the competitor objection.

conversation_arc.label: End
conversation_arc.summary: The call ends with interest but no confirmed handoff.
details: Meeting owner and time are missing.

human_experience.label: Human experience
human_experience.summary: The buyer is engaged, but uncertainty increases when pricing and competitor comparison get abstract.
details: Buyer does not commit to a meeting time.

agent_behavior.label: Agent behavior
agent_behavior.summary: The agent captures qualification data but behaves more like a pitch bot than a junior SDR after objection.

trust_limits.label: CRM outcome not attached
trust_limits.summary: The report infers handoff failure from transcript because CRM task creation is not attached.

transcript.text: Hi Maya, you requested pricing for SpectrumOps. Is now still a good time for two quick questions?
Sure, we are comparing a few tools and need something this quarter.
The concern is your competitor already includes analytics in their plan.
Our platform is very powerful and has a number of features across analytics, routing, reporting, and automation...
I think maybe I need to see actual numbers for a ten-person team.

reports.scenario: Outbound payment reminder for a failed subscription payment.
reports.expected_user_goal: Understand the payment issue and choose a safe next step.

executive_summary.overall_diagnosis: The agent explained the failed payment, but moved too quickly into collection language and did not safely handle hardship or escalation.
top_risks: Human asks for agent but AI continues
executive_summary.recommended_next_action: Audit escalation policy and payment-language guardrails before using this flow for outbound calls.

findings.title: Human requested a human agent but AI continued
findings.claim: The user asked for a person after mentioning hardship, and the AI continued the payment script.
findings.impact: This creates user harm and compliance risk in a sensitive billing conversation.
findings.likely_cause: Escalation intent was not prioritized over the collection flow.
findings.suggested_next_check: Make hardship and human-agent requests hard stops that route immediately.

findings.title: Payment explanation is procedurally correct but emotionally unsafe
findings.claim: The AI stated the failed payment and retry policy but did not acknowledge the user's hardship.
findings.impact: Correct facts can still create a bad experience when empathy and escalation are missing.
findings.likely_cause: The prompt optimizes for payment recovery before user-state recognition.
findings.suggested_next_check: Add an empathy-plus-options response before payment collection language.

findings.title: Compliance review is required for outbound payment language
findings.claim: The agent used payment reminder language in an outbound call without showing consent, disclosure, or escalation state in the report context.
findings.impact: Payment calls carry legal and reputational risk if automation scripts are not reviewed.
findings.likely_cause: The sample report lacks compliance metadata such as consent source and approved script version.
findings.suggested_next_check: Attach consent source and approved-script metadata before using billing reports for go/no-go decisions.

conversation_arc.label: Beginning
conversation_arc.summary: The AI states the payment reason clearly but needs stronger disclosure context.

conversation_arc.label: Middle
conversation_arc.summary: The user introduces hardship, and the agent continues policy-first.

conversation_arc.label: End
conversation_arc.summary: The user requests a human, but escalation does not complete.

human_experience.label: Human experience
human_experience.summary: The human moves from confusion to hardship to escalation request.

agent_behavior.label: Agent behavior
agent_behavior.summary: The agent is factually coherent but fails the sensitive-conversation handling requirement.

trust_limits.label: Compliance metadata missing
trust_limits.summary: Consent source, approved script version, and jurisdiction are not attached to the sample.

transcript.text: Hi, this is a reminder that your subscription payment did not go through yesterday.
I did not know that. What happens now?
I lost my card and I am dealing with a family emergency.
Can I please talk to a person about this?
I can take a new card now or schedule another retry for tomorrow.
